chief_complaint: A 68-year-old man presented with angina and hemodynamic instability. An 81-year-old woman presented with pulmonary embolism and systolic dysfunction with apical hypokinesia after thrombectomy.
history: The first patient was a 68-year-old man with no coronary lesions. The second patient was an 81-year-old woman with pulmonary embolism. Both patients had systolic dysfunction and apical or basal hypokinesia.
exam: Vital signs were not provided. Physical examination findings were not described.
labs: ECG showed prominent T waves in V2-V4. Ventriculogram showed systolic dysfunction and basal hypokinesia in the first patient. After thrombectomy, the second patient showed systolic dysfunction with apical hypokinesia.
correct_diagnosis: Stress cardiomyopathy triggered by pulmonary embolism and reverse Takotsubo
differentials: Stress cardiomyopathy triggered by pulmonary embolism and reverse Takotsubo
correct_ranking: Stress cardiomyopathy triggered by pulmonary embolism and reverse Takotsubo
